explanation: The code prompts the user to enter two integers, which represent the number of rows and columns for a 2D matrix. It then dynamically allocates memory for this matrix using a custom memory management function called 'mymalloc'. After allocating memory, the program fills each element of the matrix with the row index and subsequently prints the matrix to the console. Additionally, error handling is implemented to manage memory allocation failures.
name: 2009-05-31_unnamed
subject: Dynamic Allocation of 2D Array
summary: This project implements dynamic allocation for a 2D array in C, allowing the user to specify the dimensions of the matrix.
